policies.title: Shipping Policy
policies.body_html:
Shipping Policy

We roast and ship orders Monday through Thursday from our Portland, Oregon roastery. Orders placed before 11:00 AM PT typically ship the same business day; orders placed after that ship the next roasting day. Most US orders arrive within 2-5 business days via USPS Priority Mail or UPS Ground.

Domestic shipping rates

  • Orders under $40: $6.95 flat-rate USPS Priority Mail
  • Free shipping on US orders over $40
  • Subscription bags: free shipping always, regardless of order total

Roasted-on-demand

We do not pre-roast inventory. Every bag ships within 48 hours of roast, often within 24. Your bag is roasted for you, not pulled from a shelf. The trade-off is that we cannot offer same-day shipping; the upside is freshness no large roaster can match.

International shipping

We ship to Canada, the UK, and the EU. International rates are calculated at checkout based on weight and destination. Customers are responsible for any customs duties or local taxes assessed on import.

Holiday shipping

December cutoffs for guaranteed delivery before December 24 are posted on the Holiday Shipping page each November.

policies.title: Refund & Return Policy
policies.body_html:
Refund & Return Policy

If your order arrives damaged, mis-shipped, or simply not what you expected, contact us at hello@fulcrumcoffee.example within 14 days of delivery and we will make it right. We will refund or replace any unopened, unused product at our discretion, including equipment in original packaging. Refunds are issued to the original payment method within 5-7 business days of receiving the returned item. Shipping fees on the original order are non-refundable except where the issue was a Fulcrum error.

policies.title: Privacy Policy
policies.body_html:
Privacy Policy

Fulcrum Coffee Co ("we", "us") respects your privacy. This page summarizes what data we collect, how we use it, and your rights.

What we collect

  • Your name, email, shipping address, and order history when you place an order or create an account.
  • Anonymous web analytics (page views, referrer, browser type) via a privacy-respecting analytics provider that does not set persistent identifiers.
  • Email-marketing preferences if you opt in to our newsletter or post-purchase emails.

How we use it

To fulfill your orders, send you transactional emails (order confirmation, shipping updates), and - only with your consent - to send a low-volume newsletter about new origins and brewing tips.

Sharing

We do not sell your data. We share order data with our shipping carrier (USPS or UPS) and our payment processor (Shopify Payments) strictly to fulfill the order. We do not share data with marketing or advertising third parties.

Your rights

Email hello@fulcrumcoffee.example to request a copy of your data or to delete your account. We respond within 30 days.

policies.title: Terms of Service
policies.body_html:
Terms of Service

By placing an order with Fulcrum Coffee Co, you agree to these terms. They are deliberately short.

1. Orders & pricing

All prices are listed in US dollars. We may change prices, product availability, and origin lineup at any time without notice. We reserve the right to refuse or cancel any order, including orders that appear fraudulent or that exceed reasonable household quantities.

2. Subscriptions

Subscription orders renew on the cadence selected at checkout (weekly, monthly, or quarterly). You may pause, skip, or cancel at any time from your account dashboard. We charge the original payment method on the day of each renewal. Cancellations made after a renewal charge but before the order has shipped will be refunded.

3. Intellectual property

All content on this site, including photography, copy, and the Fulcrum mark, is owned by Fulcrum Coffee Co or our content partners. You may not reproduce or republish it without permission.

4. Limitation of liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Fulcrum's liability for any claim arising from your use of the site or purchase is limited to the amount paid for the relevant order.

5. Governing law

These terms are governed by the laws of the State of Oregon, USA.

policies.title: Frequently Asked Questions
policies.body_html:
FAQ

How fresh is the coffee?

Every bag ships within 48 hours of roast (often 24). The roasted-on date is printed on the bag. We recommend resting whole bean coffee 5-10 days post-roast before brewing for the best flavor.

Do you sell pre-ground coffee?

We sell whole bean only. Pre-ground coffee oxidizes within minutes; whole bean stays good for weeks. If you don't yet own a grinder, our Bean + Book bundle includes a recommendation in the included guide.

How should I store my coffee?

In an airtight container at room temperature, away from light, heat, and moisture. Do not refrigerate or freeze unless you're storing a large quantity for more than a month - and even then, freeze in airtight portions and thaw before grinding.

What's the difference between washed, natural, and honey processing?

Washed coffees are de-pulped and fermented before drying - they tend to be cleaner and more acidic. Natural coffees are dried whole, on the cherry - they tend to be sweeter and fruitier. Honey processing leaves some cherry mucilage on during drying - a hybrid. The processing method shapes the cup as much as the origin.

How long does a 250g bag last?

For one person drinking one pour-over a day, about 12-15 days. For a household of two with daily espresso plus drip, often a week.

Do you offer wholesale or cafe accounts?

We work with a small number of cafes and offices. Email wholesale@fulcrumcoffee.example with a brief description of your business and weekly volume estimate.

Can I tour the roastery?

Public tours are paused while we expand the production floor. We expect to resume in late 2026.

What if my order arrives damaged?

Email hello@fulcrumcoffee.example within 14 days and we'll replace it. See our Refund Policy for full details.

policies.title: Contact Us
policies.body_html:
Contact Fulcrum Coffee Co

We try to answer every email within one business day. We're a small team, and we genuinely enjoy talking about coffee, so don't hesitate to write.

Email

  • General questions, order issues, returns: hello@fulcrumcoffee.example
  • Wholesale & cafe accounts: wholesale@fulcrumcoffee.example
  • Press & partnerships: press@fulcrumcoffee.example

Roastery & shipping address

Fulcrum Coffee Co
2412 NW Industrial Way
Portland, OR 97210
United States

Hours

The roastery is staffed Monday through Thursday, 7:00 AM to 4:00 PM Pacific Time. We are not open to the public; please email before visiting.

Social

We post brew videos and harvest updates on Instagram (@fulcrumcoffee) and a long-form journal at fulcrumcoffee.example/journal.

policies.title: About Fulcrum Coffee Co
policies.body_html:
About Fulcrum

Fulcrum Coffee Co was founded in 2019 in Portland, Oregon by a small group of home brewers who got tired of buying coffee from roasters who refused to tell us anything useful about how it was grown, processed, or roasted. We started Fulcrum to be the roaster we wanted to buy from: traceable to the lot, transparent on pricing, generous with brewing guidance, and uninterested in marketing flourishes that don't make the coffee taste better.

What we believe

We believe specialty coffee is a craft, not a luxury. We believe brewing guidance belongs on the bag, not behind a paywall. We believe naming the farmer matters more than naming the country. We believe that if you don't know what you're drinking, the roaster has failed at their job - not you.

How we source

We import green coffee through three relationships-first partners (one based in Portland, one in Brooklyn, one in Hamburg) who publish farmgate prices and visit the farms. We do not buy spot-market coffee. We do not buy lots we cannot trace to a single farm or washing station. Our 12 origin lineup rotates seasonally based on harvest cycles in each country - so the lot of "Ethiopia Yirgacheffe" you buy in May is genuinely a different lot from the one you bought in November.

How we roast

We roast on a 12kg Probat drum roaster, in batch sizes that let our roasters track each profile by hand. Every batch is sample-cupped before release. Anything that doesn't meet the cupping bar gets re-roasted as our staff coffee or composted. We do not blend lots to hide defects. We do not buy mass-graded coffee.

Brand voice

We try to write the way we'd talk to you across the bar at our roastery. Patient, curious, slightly nerdy, allergic to hype.

Free shipping

We offer free shipping on US orders over $50, plus free shipping on every subscription bag regardless of order size, because subscribers have already committed to the relationship.

Where we're going

We expect to add direct-trade lots from two new origins in 2026 and to release a long-form video brewing guide alongside our existing recipe cards. The roastery floor expansion finishes in Q3.
